overview: Cast cheap instants and sorceries to generate a relentless army of 1/1 haste Wizard tokens, then sacrifice them to pump your commander or a secondary threat for lethal. This deck wins by out-casting opponents with instant-speed interaction and overwhelming board presence before control or combo can stabilize.
win_conditions: The commander itself (pumped via sacrifice), or a separate big threat fueled by token sacrifice.
Lethal comes from a single massive swing or repeated pump-and-swing turns once you have consistent sacrifice outlets.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including basics or fetchable duals), at least one mana acceleration piece (Sol Ring, Arcane Signet, or a ramp spell), and 2-3 cheap instants/sorceries or a sacrifice outlet.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan if you have 3+ lands with no acceleration, only expensive spells, or no way to cast your commander early.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a land, cast Sol Ring/Arcane Signet.
Turn 2: Cast a cheap instant or sorcery to trigger the commander, creating a 1/1 haste token.
Sequence mana to ensure you can cast multiple spells per turn as soon as possible, prioritizing cheap, efficient spells that draw cards or deal damage.
mid_game: Prioritize casting instants/sorceries over hoarding cards.
Use mana rocks and ramp to maintain a high spell density.
Don't overcommit to pumping if you lack sacrifice outlets, and always preserve mana for flash protection on your commander.
Commit threats when you have enough tokens to trade favorably or when the opponent has no removal or sweepers.
Hold back if they have board wipes or targeted removal.
Use flash to protect your commander and surprise-block or attack.
late_game: The commander itself (pumped via sacrifice), or a separate big threat fueled by token sacrifice.
Lethal comes from a single massive swing or repeated pump-and-swing turns once you have consistent sacrifice outlets.
When you have 3+ tokens, a reliable sacrifice outlet, and the opponent has no sweepers or removal in hand.
Also commit when you can close the game before their next turn or when you can trade your tokens for their key permanents.

sequencing_tips: Common mistake: Over-pumping without enough sacrifice outlets.
Remember that the commander's ability is a sacrifice outlet itself, but you'll need more for consistent pressure.
Prismari spells often have additional effects, so prioritize those that draw cards or deal damage.
Flash and haste synergy is key, and always protect your commander with counterspells or protection spells.

matchups.advice: Use flash, split second (if any), or cast spells in response to their counterspells. Use cheap spells to trade up. Rely on instant-speed interaction to clear the way and pressure before they can cast their own spells.
Apply early pressure to race them. Use counterspells or removal to stop their mana dorks/rituals. Don't let them stabilize; force them to interact with your board while you push for lethal.
Use cheap removal and counterspells to stall. Build a board of tokens to trade into their attackers. Use pump effects to survive combat and stabilize before going on the offensive.

Out-value them with card advantage and cheap spells. Use flash to bypass their removal windows. Push for lethal before they can find their win condition, and use instant-speed interaction to disrupt their plan.

It's a race to cast more spells and find better sacrifice outlets. Prioritize counterspells, targeted removal, and card draw. Win by disrupting their key spells, out-resourcing them in the late game, or finding a unique finisher they can't answer.

common_threats: Watch for board wipes (Wrath of God, etc.), targeted removal on your commander, and counterspells.
Note when opponents tap out for big spells versus holding up mana for interaction, and track how many instants/sorceries they've cast to gauge their hand size.
